warnings: The Goldstone bosons (3 real scalars) are absorbed as longitudinal components of the massive gauge bosons.
The quartic coupling lambda is radiatively generated; it is never a free parameter.
No fermions are included (minimal for FOPT+GW only).
Stable dark sector contributes to Delta N_eff and may be constrained by BBN/CMB.
Model is not novel (arXiv:2109.11558 studies the exact same model).
approximations: High-temperature expansion of the one-loop finite-temperature effective potential.
CW mechanism at one loop; higher-order corrections neglected.
Radial mode mass-squared m_chi^2 = beta_lambda * chi0^2 at one-loop order.

particle_content.name: Phi
particle_content.notes: Complex scalar SU(2) doublet, Phi = (phi^+, phi^0)^T. After SSB, one real scalar (radial mode / pseudo-dilaton chi) remains; 3 Goldstones absorbed as longitudinal modes.

particle_content.name: W^a_mu
particle_content.notes: 3 SU(2) gauge bosons, 9 dof in broken phase (3 massive vectors x 3 polarizations each).

lagrangian_terms.name: gauge_kinetic
lagrangian_terms.notes: Pure SU(2) Yang-Mills. W^a_{munu} = partial_mu W^a_nu - partial_nu W^a_mu + g * epsilon^{abc} W^b_mu W^c_nu.

lagrangian_terms.name: scalar_kinetic
lagrangian_terms.notes: D_mu = partial_mu + i * g * W^a_mu * tau^a with tau^a = sigma^a/2.

lagrangian_terms.name: scalar_potential
lagrangian_terms.notes: Classically scale-invariant quartic. Lambda is radiatively generated via CW; not a free parameter.

free_parameters.name: g
free_parameters.notes: SU(2)_X gauge coupling. Perturbative (g < 4*pi). Determines gauge boson mass-over-vev ratio gb = g/2.

free_parameters.name: chi0
free_parameters.notes: Scalar VEV at zero temperature. Sets the scale of symmetry breaking and all particle masses.

beta_lambda.description: CW beta_lambda coefficient for the quartic coupling. Fixed by gauge boson contributions (9 real dof, each with gb = g/2).
beta_lambda.derivation: CW relation: beta_lambda = (1/(8*pi^2)) * (sum_B n_B * gb^4 - sum_F n_F * gf^4). Three massive SU(2) gauge bosons, each with 3 polarizations = 9 real dof, each with gb = g/2. No fermions. beta_lambda = 9 * (g/2)^4 / (8*pi^2) = 9*g^4/(128*pi^2).

m_chi2.description: Mass squared of the radial (pseudo-dilaton) mode at zero temperature

gb_scalar.description: Mass-over-vev ratio for the radial scalar (auto-computed by the backend)

delta_V.description: Vacuum energy density difference between symmetric and broken phases at zero temperature

ssb_pattern.order_parameter: chi (radial mode of the complex scalar doublet Phi)
ssb_pattern.notes: SU(2)_X completely broken by the VEV. All three gauge bosons become massive with equal mass m_W = g * chi0 / 2.

vev_convention.notes: Phi expanded around background as (G1 + i*G2, (chi + h + i*G3)) / sqrt(2). G_{1,2,3} are Goldstones, h is physical radial mode, chi is classical background.

tree_level_potential.notes: No quadratic mass term due to classical scale invariance. Lambda is radiatively generated; not required as backend input.

field_dependent_masses.notes: Three SU(2) gauge bosons, each with field-dependent mass m_W^2 = g^2 * chi^2 / 4. Total 3 x 3 = 9 dof (massive vectors).
Physical radial mode (pseudo-dilaton) mass from CW mechanism. beta_lambda = 9*g^4/(128*pi^2). Scalar gb is auto-computed by the backend.

thermal_masses.particle: W^a longitudinal (Debye mass for daisy resummation)
thermal_masses.notes: Longitudinal (electric) Debye mass for SU(2) gauge bosons. Formula: m_D^2 = g^2 T^2 (N/3 + N_s/12) with N=2, N_s=4 real scalars (one complex doublet). Gives m_D^2 = g^2*T^2.
Thermal mass for the scalar radial mode from gauge boson loops: m_D^2 = C_f * g^2 * T^2 / 4 with C_f = 3/4 for SU(2) fundamental. Gives m_D^2 = 3*g^2*T^2/16.
Goldstone bosons have the same thermal mass as the radial scalar from gauge interactions. In the symmetric phase, all scalar components share this thermal mass.

expression_symbols.description: Background value of the scalar radial field
One-loop beta function coefficient for the quartic coupling
Mass squared of the radial scalar mode
Mass-over-vev ratio for the scalar, equal to sqrt(beta_lambda)
Vacuum energy difference at zero temperature

assumptions: Classically scale-invariant at tree level: no explicit mass scales in the Lagrangian.
Radiative symmetry breaking via the Coleman-Weinberg mechanism.
beta_lambda > 0: satisfied because only bosons (gauge bosons) contribute.
Perturbative regime (g < 4*pi) for one-loop CW validity.
No U(1) factor: pure SU(2)_X without hypercharge.
No fermions: minimal choice for FOPT+GW production.
High-temperature expansion valid for the parameter space of interest.
Gaussian approximation for false-vacuum fraction as implemented in the backend.
Stable dark sector: no SM coupling, contributes to Delta N_eff (constraint to be evaluated downstream).

backend_compatibility.notes: Single-field conformal model with all masses proportional to one scalar vev and no tree-level mass term. Exactly matches the backend requirements. The three SU(2) gauge bosons (9 dof) expand the backend's single-U(1) example (3 dof) to the non-Abelian case. The backend's internal get_beta_lambda() computes the correct CW coefficient.
